runtime_proof.note: Pure TypeScript enforcement — ExpirationRecord is a static value. No live DB required. Expiration decisions are deterministic from (issuedAt, kind) alone and are replay-safe.
queries: APPROVAL_WINDOW_SECONDS includes all required window kinds (dual-auth, operator-action, member-promotion)
computeExpiresAt produces correct future timestamp for dual-auth
computeExpiresAt is deterministic — same inputs produce equal outputs
isApprovalExpired returns false when within window
isApprovalExpired returns true when past expiry
BOUNDARY: isApprovalExpired returns true at exactly expiry time (fail-closed boundary)
ADVERSARIAL: assertApprovalNotExpired throws ApprovalExpiredError when expired (dual-auth)
ADVERSARIAL: assertApprovalNotExpired throws for operator-action when expired
ADVERSARIAL: assertApprovalNotExpired throws for member-promotion when expired
ADVERSARIAL: assertApprovalNotExpired throws at exactly expiry time (boundary fail-closed)
createExpirationRecord produces a frozen ExpirationRecord with correct fields
createExpirationRecord captures expiredAt when approval was recorded as expired
ExpirationRecord is frozen — mutations throw TypeError in strict mode
replayExpirationChain is deterministic — same inputs produce equal ExpirationRecords
ApprovalExpiredError has correct name, code, kind, and expiresAt

row_counts.note: No database tables modified — expiration enforcement is pure TypeScript with no DB dependency.

receipts.note: Pure TypeScript enforcement — ExpirationRecord values are deterministic; no Supabase interaction
receipts.gap_closed: INIT-2.4.3 — approval expiration was implicit in dual-auth TTL only; now all governance approval windows have explicit, deterministic, fail-closed expiration enforcement
